description: Enforces centralized external API access through the shared HTTP client wrappers.
rationale: Outbound HTTP behavior should be centralized for consistent headers, timeouts, error handling, and telemetry.
Direct fetch and ad hoc HTTP clients make browser/server behavior diverge and bypass shared policy decisions.
A narrow set of approved wrappers keeps transport changes localized.
notes: Use packages/core/src/api-client.ts for traced generic outbound HTTP access.
Use apps/web/src/lib/browser-api-client.ts for browser-facing API access that returns ApiResponse envelopes.
Tests are excluded so they can stub or exercise low-level transports directly.

rules.message: Use the centralized API clients instead of direct fetch(...) calls
Use the centralized API clients instead of globalThis.fetch(...)
Use the centralized API clients instead of XMLHttpRequest
Use the centralized API clients instead of ad hoc HTTP client libraries
